angle: The adherence (AI steering) and logic-reading (human/machine readability) jobs the 029 benchmark never touched. The benchmark measured only retrieval and the requestQuality envelope; 005's adherence and logic levers (A4 schema gate, A7 EARS/REQ_COVERAGE, A1 content scorer, novel contradiction detection) all remain scaffolded-not-built. I read the real validate.sh, validator-registry.json, post-save-review.ts, confidence-scoring.ts, search-results.ts formatter, and the child specs to confirm what ships today versus what 005 designed but never wired, and to find the one adherence gap the benchmark surfaced that 005 has no lever for.

The adherence (AI steering) and logic-reading (human/machine readability) jobs the 029 benchmark never touched. The benchmark measured only retrieval and the requestQuality envelope; 005's adherence and logic levers (A4 schema gate, A7 EARS/REQ_COVERAGE, A1 content scorer, novel contradiction detection) all remain scaffolded-not-built. I read the real validate.sh, validator-registry.json, post-save-review.ts, confidence-scoring.ts, search-results.ts formatter, and the child specs to confirm what ships today versus what 005 designed but never wired, and to find the one adherence gap the benchmark surfaced that 005 has no lever for.
title: Make the requestQuality envelope a contract the agent must render, not an optional field
problem: The 029 benchmark proved the data-quality ENVELOPE is the actual runtime adherence surface: every model honored cite-iff-good at 1.0 and verdicts were identical whenever emitted, so the ONLY cross-model failure was envelope FIELD-DROP — gpt-5.5 at medium dropped requestQuality on 3 generic queries, Kimi dropped it on 2 aligned queries (029/benchmark-results.md:67-69,79; 029/implementation-summary.md:59). When the field drops, the consuming agent loses the cite-iff-good signal entirely and is back to ungated citation. The envelope is computed server-side and gated only by isResultConfidenceEnabled() (search-results.ts:832); deriveCitationPolicy returns a policy whenever requestQuality exists (search-results.ts:368-369) but nothing in the response asserts the agent actually surfaced it. Every 005 adherence lever (A7 EARS, constraint tier, A1 content scorer) targets AUTHORED spec docs at write time — none touches the live retrieval envelope the agent reads at query time, so this gap is genuinely unowned by 005's current slate.
evidence: 029/benchmark-results.md:67-69,79 (field-drop is the only variance); 029/implementation-summary.md:59,107 (variant axis conflates the drop); search-results.ts:368-369,832,843,852-857 (envelope computed server-side, no render assertion); confidence-scoring.ts:100,433 (assessRequestQuality is the source of the label). All 005 adherence child specs (007-a7) target authored docs, not the envelope.
proposal: Add a minimal envelope-fidelity assertion to the search response: when requestQuality is computed, stamp a required 'envelopeContract' marker (e.g. contractVersion + a list of the 7 expected slots) into the response metadata, and document in the /memory:search command card that the consuming agent MUST echo requestQuality.label and citationPolicy in its answer. Pair it with a lightweight post-hoc check in the benchmark/eval path that flags any dispatch whose rendered output omits the verdict. This is a runtime-adherence lever, the sibling 005 never built for the query-time reader.
benefit: Closes the one adherence failure mode the 144-cell benchmark actually surfaced. Turns the model-robust cite-iff-good signal into a contract that survives a terse or over-exploring driver, so the steering job holds regardless of which model drives /memory:search.
risk: Low blast radius (additive metadata field + doc/card change). Risk is scope creep into the calibration code — keep it to the envelope contract, not the score. A render-side assertion cannot force a model to comply, only flag non-compliance, so it is detective not preventive.

title: Ship A4 schema-gate warn-to-error now: the corpus is already clean of the only bypass
problem: 005 named A4 (promote graph-metadata/description JSON-schema validation from warn to error) as its ONE measured unconditional GO, gated only on re-measuring the corpus to zero schema failures and zero grandfathered packets (spec.md:163,172; research/research.md:21,108). I confirmed the gate is still warn today: validate.sh:83 sets RULE_SEVERITY_GRAPH_METADATA_PRESENT="warn", and the legacy_grandfathered bypass still exists (validate.sh:175-180). I also confirmed the bypass-removal blast radius the research claimed: grep for legacy_grandfathered:true across .opencode/specs returns 0 packets, so dropping the dormant bypass breaks nothing. This is a pure adherence/logic (machine-readability) lever — invalid metadata JSON silently passes today, leaving malformed importance_tier/status/content_type values that the AI and graph traversal read as truth.
evidence: spec.md:163,172 (A4 = the one measured GO, flip-gated on re-measure-to-zero); validate.sh:83 (still warn), validate.sh:175-180 (legacy_grandfathered bypass live); grep legacy_grandfathered:true over .opencode/specs = 0 packets (bypass deletes with zero blast radius); 004-a4 child spec exists and is scaffolded. research/research.md:21,108 (24/16 invalid-file census).
proposal: Execute the documented four-beat migration for the schema gate (WARN already live -> BACKFILL the 16 live invalid graph files -> RE-MEASURE to zero -> flip RULE_SEVERITY_GRAPH_METADATA to error and delete the legacy_grandfathered branch). The research already counted the failing set (16 live, 24 including archives, vs the stale census of 11) so the backfill scope is known. This is the lowest-risk highest-certainty 005 build and the benchmark gives no reason to defer it — it touches validation, not the calibration the benchmark questioned.
benefit: Hard-stops malformed metadata JSON from entering the corpus, so the logic-reading job (graph traversal + AI reading importance_tier/status) gets a guaranteed-valid substrate. Highest certainty of any 005 lever — surface counted, mechanism shipped, zero re-index, zero retrieval risk.
risk: Backfill must reach zero before the flip or the legacy corpus breaks (the four-beat discipline exists precisely for this). The 16-file backfill is the real work; the flip itself is one severity change plus deleting a dead branch. Must re-measure live, not trust the stale 11-file census.

title: Build A7's REQ_COVERAGE gate by cloning the shipped AC_COVERAGE rule — adherence traceability with a proven pattern
problem: 005's adherence story for authored docs rests on A7 (EARS grammar, always/ask-first/never constraint tier, REQ_COVERAGE gate), all scaffolded but unbuilt. The spec-REQ-to-tasks linkage gap is real and unguarded: tasks.md lists T001-style IDs with no requirement back-reference, so nothing verifies every authored requirement is actually built (007-a7 spec.md:64,109). The gate shape A7 needs already ships — I confirmed check-ac-coverage.sh exists and AC_COVERAGE is registered in validator-registry.json:51-62 with the full SPECKIT_AC_COVERAGE/_ENFORCE/_FLOOR flag triad — but REQ_COVERAGE, EARS, and any CONTENT_QUALITY rule are absent from the registry (only AC_COVERAGE matched). So the highest-value adherence lever is a clone of a working rule, not green-field.
evidence: 007-a7 spec.md:64 (linkage gap unguarded, tasks.md.tmpl:54-77 has no REQ column), 007-a7 spec.md:109,111,138 (AC_COVERAGE is the proven clone source at validator-registry.json:51-62); confirmed live: check-ac-coverage.sh exists, validator-registry.json:51-62 carries AC_COVERAGE + its 3 flags, and grep for REQ_COVERAGE/EARS/CONTENT_QUALITY in the registry returned only the AC_COVERAGE lines.
proposal: Build the REQ_COVERAGE half of A7 first (defer the softer EARS linter): clone check-ac-coverage.sh to check-req-coverage.sh, retarget from checklist AC rows to a REQ-reference marker on tasks.md rows, register it in validator-registry.json mirroring the AC_COVERAGE entry, default-off and warn. Add the REQ-reference marker to tasks.md.tmpl so the gate has a canonical linkage location. This makes 'every requirement maps to a build task' a checkable adherence property, which today nothing enforces.
benefit: Gives the adherence reader a machine-checkable requirement-to-task traceability rail, reusing a shipped, CI-tested pattern so build risk is low. Floor-bypassing (touches validation, not vectors) so it ships on cost with no prod-mode-recall tax.
risk: Default-off/warn keeps the legacy corpus safe, but the legacy specs lack the REQ marker so coverage is unmeasurable until tasks.md.tmpl is adopted — the gate must degrade to advisory, not hard-fail, on docs with no marker (007-a7 spec.md:170). EARS auto-rewrite must stay suggest-only (it would mutate authored prose, research/research.md:83). Lower priority than A4 because it requires a template-adoption ramp before it bites.

title: Stand up the report-only cross-doc contradiction detector for the logic-consistency reader
problem: The corpus has detectors for shape, frontmatter, and within-packet cross-surface coherence, but nothing checks whether two distinct docs describing the same entity actually AGREE on the facts — a claim and its later correction can both sit live in the index (019 spec.md:62). The benchmark's kubernetes false-relevance is a retrieval-calibration story (confidence-scoring.ts thresholds 0.8/0.7/0.4 at lines 39-40,78), well-covered by the calibration angle; but its deeper cousin — a stale or contradicted CLAIM surviving in the corpus — is invisible to every shipped gate and to retrieval itself, because a contradiction is a finding, not a vector row, so no recall measurement ever surfaces it (019 spec.md:62). 005 designed this as a novel GO-on-cost item (research/research.md:74) but it is scaffolded-only.
evidence: 019 spec.md:62 (no gate checks cross-doc factual agreement; contradiction is a finding not a vector row), 019 spec.md:74-77,91-93 (entity/causal pair-gate, report-only, mounts on shared engine), research/research.md:74 (the lineage-headline novel GO-on-cost, candidate-pair-gated by shipped entity catalog + causal graph). Confirmed no shipped contradiction/entailment detector: grep over scripts returned none.
proposal: Build the 019 contradiction/staleness detector as designed: a report-only detector (fixClass:none) on the B1 sweep that pairs ONLY docs sharing an entity_catalog entity or causal edge (never all-pairs), scores each pair agree/contradict/stale via an LLM entailment check, and emits an edge-tagged finding into the existing sweep/doctor report channel. It mounts on the shared safe-fix engine (026) and B1 sweep (011), so it is one registry entry plus one detector file, not new infrastructure.
benefit: Serves the logic-consistency reader nothing else touches — surfaces a stale or contradicted claim before a reader trusts the wrong one. Floor-bypassing (emits findings, not vectors) so no re-index or prod-mode tax, and it is the strongest defense against the deeper version of the spurious-confidence problem the benchmark hinted at.
risk: Highest effort of the four (depends on 026 engine + 011 sweep landing first, both unbuilt). LLM entailment can false-positive or hallucinate a contradiction (019 spec.md:138) — mitigated by report-only + confidence-tagging + the entity/causal pair-gate keeping volume low. All-pairs scoring would blow up cost/time, so the pair-gate is load-bearing, not optional.
